steps: 1. Launch the application. 2. Wait for the splash screen. 3. Swipe through onboarding (ON_01B-ON_01D). 4. Reach ON_01E and tap "Get Started!". 5. On the home screen, connect the printer and select an image to reach Print Preview. 6. Ensure printer is in firmware update mode. 7. Tap "Print".
cases.expected: The "Please Wait..." pop-up is displayed with a red warning icon, the instructional text "Please wait for the firmware update to finish before starting a print job.", an "OK" button, and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The print job is blocked until the firmware update is completed.

verifications.expected: The "Please Wait..." pop-up is displayed with a red warning icon, the instructional text "Please wait for the firmware update to finish before starting a print job.", an "OK" button, and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The print job is blocked until the firmware update is completed.

cases.description: Intercept Print Action During Active Firmware Update
steps: 1. Launch the application.
2. Wait for splash screen completion.
3. Swipe to continue and complete onboarding steps (ON_01B → ON_01D).
4. Reach ON_01E onboarding screen.
5. Locate and verify the Get Started! button.
6. Tap the Get Started! button.
7. Ensure printer firmware update is actively in progress.
8. Navigate to the Print Preview screen.
9. Tap the Print button.
cases.expected: The print job is blocked and not sent to the queue. A blocking overlay popup appears with a clock icon, header "Please Wait....", and subtext "Please wait for firmware installation to finish before sending a print job." an "OK" button, and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner

verifications.expected: The print job is blocked and not sent to the queue. cases.description: Verify No Paper Tray Error during firmware update
steps: 1. Launch the application and wait for splash screen to complete. 2. Complete onboarding by swiping until Get Started appears. 3. Tap Get Started → Sign Up Later. 4. User lands on Home screen. 5. Tap Printer icon. 6. User is redirected to No Printer Added screen. 7. Tap Added a New Printer. 8. Select HP Sprocket Studio Plus printer. 9. Proceed through Wi-Fi setup flow and complete printer connection. 10. Navigate to Printer Settings screen. 11. Remove paper tray from printer (simulate no tray condition). 12. Tap Update Available button. 13. System attempts firmware update validation. 14. Verify DE_08L error screen is displayed.
cases.expected: Error message is displayed: A "No Paper Tray" error popup is displayed with the message "Please attach the paper tray (loaded with paper) to complete the firmware update." The popup includes a "Try Again" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The firmware update cannot continue until the paper tray is attached.

cases.description: Verify Try Again functionality after resolving hardware issue
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding flow. 2. Navigate to Printer Settings screen. 3. Trigger any firmware error condition (paper/jam/ribbon). 4. Verify corresponding error screen is displayed. 5. Fix the hardware issue (insert paper / clear jam / insert ribbon). 6. Tap Try Again button on error screen. 7. System re-validates printer status. 8. Verify firmware process resumes and navigates to HPS_FW_04 While Installing screen if issue is resolved.
cases.expected: System retries firmware update successfully after issue is resolved and continues installation flow.

cases.description: Verify Firmware Update Available popup is displayed
steps: 1. Make sure 200 printer is powered on.
2. Launch the application.
3. Wait for splash screen to complete.
4. Swipe onboarding screens until final screen.
5. Tap 'Get Started'.

22. Tap 'Update Available!' button.
